files.title: The Nordic Nerd — Operations System Summary
files.content:
# The Nordic Nerd — Operations System Summary

**Built:** 2026-05-27
**Owner:** Adam | Winter Haven, FL
**Goal:** Replace W2 income via The Nordic Nerd (Polk County contractor agency) within 12 months
**Files created:** 47 (this file + 46 deliverables)

---

## What's in here

This is the complete starting kit for The Nordic Nerd's operating system. Everything was generated from the brief — no third-party signups happened, no DNS was modified, no money moved. Every file is yours to read, edit, and execute against.

The structure follows the foundational principle: **work top-down, week-by-week.** Don't jump ahead. Don't try to do everything in week 1.

---

## Recommended order of use

### Week 1 — Read, decide, file paperwork

1. Read `/00-Account-Hygiene/` (all 3 files, ~25 min total)
2. File the FL LLC on SunBiz.org → get EIN from IRS.gov
3. Book a 30-min consult with a FL startup attorney using the prompts in `ip-protection-notes.md`
4. Print `bright-line-rules.md` and stick it next to your monitor
5. Pick your `{{PHYSICAL_ADDRESS}}` solution (PO Box recommended)
6. Open `/01-Stack-Setup/tools-and-accounts.md` and start the Tier 1 accounts (Cloudflare, Google Workspace primary, Mercury/Relay)

### Week 2 — Foundation infra

1. Register the 3 domains per `dns-and-domain-plan.md`
2. Configure DNS for primary domain
3. Provision the Hetzner VPS per `n8n-deployment.md`
4. Boot n8n + Documenso + Caddy on the VPS
5. Set up Google Workspace primary on `thenordicnerd.com`
6. Begin Workspace cold-tenant signup on `nordicnerdhq.com` (per `google-workspace-setup.md`)

### Week 3 — Outbound stack

1. Set up Smartlead per `smartlead-setup.md` — connect inboxes, START warmup (clock starts now)
2. Set up Apify, Hunter.io, MillionVerifier
3. Configure HubSpot per `hubspot-setup.md` — build pipeline, add custom properties
4. Configure Stripe per `stripe-setup.md` — create 3 products, activate live mode
5. Set up Cal.com on `calendar.app.google/JheMSKoDMrWkiixy6`
6. Set up MailerLite, Notion, Wave

### Week 4 — Agents + workflows + content prep

1. Import the 5 n8n workflow JSONs into your n8n instance (from `/03-Outbound-System/n8n-workflows/`)
2. Configure n8n credentials (Apify, Hunter, MillionVerifier, Smartlead, HubSpot, Stripe, Anthropic, MailerLite, Google Sheets, Slack)
3. Replace all `PLACEHOLDER_*` values in the workflow JSONs with your real IDs
4. Paste the production system prompts into the relevant HTTP nodes (from `/03-Outbound-System/personalization/` and `/03-Outbound-System/reply-handling/`)
5. Read all 5 agent rules files in `/02-Agents/` — these are your mental model for who owns what
6. Begin writing Episode 1 of the YouTube series (`/05-Content/agency-from-scratch-youtube/ep01-the-stack.md`)

### Week 5–6 — Warmup completion + dry run

1. Smartlead warmup should be hitting day 15+ — domains warming
2. First Apify scrape run manually (no Smartlead push yet) — eyeball list quality
3. Test the n8n reply-triage workflow end-to-end (send yourself a fake reply, verify draft generation)
4. Final pre-launch CAN-SPAM check using `/03-Outbound-System/compliance/can-spam-checklist.md`

### Week 7 — Soft launch

1. First 50 cold emails go out
2. Daily check using `/06-Daily-Ops/morning-review-checklist.md`
3. Tune the system based on what breaks

### Months 2+ — Cadence

- Every morning: 15-min review per the daily checklist
- Every Monday: weekly metrics rollup per `/06-Daily-Ops/weekly-metrics-template.md`
- Last Friday of each month: monthly retro per `/06-Daily-Ops/monthly-retro-template.md`
- First week of each new month: YouTube episode published + scoreboard public

---

## File inventory (46 files)

### `/00-Account-Hygiene/` (3 files, ~2,600 words)

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `account-separation-checklist.md` | 9-section checklist to separate W2 employer infra from agency infra |
| `bright-line-rules.md` | The non-negotiable "what lives where" rules, single-page poster format |
| `ip-protection-notes.md` | Lay summary of IP/legal risks + attorney consult question list |

### `/01-Stack-Setup/` (8 files, ~7,400 words)

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `tools-and-accounts.md` | Master inventory of every tool with URLs, costs, signup order |
| `dns-and-domain-plan.md` | DNS records for all 3 domains, MX/SPF/DKIM/DMARC config |
| `google-workspace-setup.md` | Two-tenant setup with 4 inboxes (1 primary + 3 cold) |
| `smartlead-setup.md` | Account, inbox connection, 14-21 day warmup ramp, campaign config |
| `apify-scrape-config.md` | Search matrix for 7 cities × 8 niches, scoring rules, filters |
| `n8n-deployment.md` | Full Docker Compose stack on Hetzner CX22 (€4.51/mo) for n8n + Documenso + Postgres + Caddy |
| `hubspot-setup.md` | Free-tier CRM setup: 6-stage retainer pipeline + custom properties + automation |
| `stripe-setup.md` | 3 products (Starter/Growth/Authority at $1.5K/$2K/$2.5K), Customer Portal, webhooks |

### `/02-Agents/` (5 files, ~5,200 words)

Five `rules.md`-style files defining the multi-agent system.

| File | Owns |
|---|---|
| `outbound-ops-agent.md` | Top of funnel: scrapes, deliverability, list hygiene, sequencer state |
| `copywriter-agent.md` | All words touching prospects/clients: sequences, subjects, first-lines, reply drafts |
| `pipeline-agent.md` | Reply → call → proposal → signed → first invoice |
| `client-delivery-agent.md` | All retainer delivery: site builds, GBP, content, monthly reports |
| `operations-agent.md` | Orchestration: daily briefings, weekly rollups, monthly retros, scoreboards |

### `/03-Outbound-System/` (15 files, ~14,300 words + 5 JSON workflows)

**Sequences** (3 files)
- `polk-contractor-sequence-v1.md` — 4-email sequence over 14 days with merge fields and rationale
- `subject-line-bank.md` — 30 subject variants organized into 6 testing categories
- `sequence-variants-by-niche.md` — Niche-specific hook variations across all 8 niches

**Personalization** (2 files)
- `first-line-system-prompt.md` — Production-grade Claude system prompt for first-line generation
- `first-line-examples.md` — 20 worked input→output examples across all niches

**Reply handling** (3 files)
- `reply-classification-prompt.md` — 6-category classifier prompt with JSON output schema
- `reply-draft-prompt.md` — Reply drafting prompt with voice DNA + escalation logic
- `escalation-triggers.md` — Tier 1/2/3/4 triggers and routing rules

**n8n workflows** (5 JSON files, all validated as parseable)
- `01-weekly-scrape.json` — Apify → dedup → quality filter → Hunter → MillionVerifier → GBP score → HubSpot check → clean list → Slack
- `02-push-to-sequencer.json` — Sheet read → Claude first-line → Smartlead lead create → HubSpot contact + deal → mark pushed
- `03-reply-triage.json` — Smartlead webhook → Claude classifier → switch (escalate/suppress/OOO/negative/positive) → optional Claude draft → Slack
- `04-booking-to-proposal.json` — Cal.com webhook + manual proposal trigger → Claude prep brief → Documenso template → HubSpot stage move
- `05-signed-to-onboarding.json` — Documenso completed → Stripe customer + subscription → MailerLite onboarding → HubSpot Won + Stripe events handler

**Compliance** (2 files)
- `can-spam-checklist.md` — 5 CAN-SPAM rules with current status, plus FL state-law notes
- `reply-monitoring-sla.md` — Response time targets by classification, escalation channels, OOO handling

### `/04-Client-Templates/` (5 files, ~8,000 words)

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `retainer-agreement-v1.md` | DRAFT — must be attorney-reviewed. 9 sections covering Term, Fees, Confidentiality, IP, Warranties, Liability, Indemnification, General Provisions. Includes Exhibit A (SOW). |
| `proposal-template.md` | Documenso template (one variant per tier) with merge fields and tier-specific deliverable blocks |
| `discovery-call-script.md` | 15-20 min call framework: questions, transition phrases, when NOT to send a proposal |
| `onboarding-checklist.md` | Day 0 → Day 30 client onboarding with auto/manual steps and 12-question intake questionnaire |
| `monthly-reporting-template.md` | Monthly report structure delivered to retainer clients with metrics tables and strategic recommendation |

### `/05-Content/` (7 files, ~8,400 words)

**YouTube — `agency-from-scratch-youtube/`** (5 files)
- `series-arc.md` — 12-episode arc, scoreboard premise, production approach, anti-patterns
- `ep01-the-stack.md` — Full outline for Month 1 episode (the tool stack walkthrough)
- `ep02-multi-agent-outbound.md` — Full outline for Month 2 episode (workflow deep-dives)
- `ep03-first-100-cold-emails.md` — Full outline for Month 3 episode (cold-email data + first close anatomy)
- `scoreboard-template.md` — Public and internal scoreboard formats with visual treatment notes

**Blog — `blog/`** (2 files)
- `content-calendar-q1.md` — 12-week calendar: 12 blog posts + 12 SEO pages mapped week-by-week
- `city-niche-page-template.md` — Reusable Nuxt Content template for `[niche] in [city] FL` pages with schema markup, performance budgets, and content quality bar

### `/06-Daily-Ops/` (3 files, ~3,300 words)

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `morning-review-checklist.md` | 15-min weekday routine: deliverability → replies → pipeline → clients → infra → today's three |
| `weekly-metrics-template.md` | Monday metric rollup template with outbound, pipeline, delivery, infra, content, W2-replacement tracker, next week's three priorities |
| `monthly-retro-template.md` | 90-min last-Friday-of-month retro template: scoreboard → what-happened → math check → keep/stop/iterate → agent updates → 3 priorities → 1 question to sit with |

---

## Choices made for you (override at will)

The brief said cosmetics could be picked. Here's what I picked and why:

| Choice | Picked | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Secondary cold domains | `nordicnerdhq.com`, `getnordicnerd.com` | Both .com (deliverability), brand-aligned, easy to spell on calls |
| VPS provider | Hetzner CX22 (€4.51/mo) | More RAM than DO $6 droplet; well-priced for n8n+Documenso |
| Cold inbox warmup | 14-day standard ramp (5→40/day per inbox) | Conservative; better safe than sorry on reputation |
| Claude model for first-lines/classification/drafts | `claude-sonnet-4-6` | Quality/cost balance; Opus reserved for high-intent reply drafts and proposal prep briefs |
| HubSpot pipeline stages | 6 stages (New Lead → Replied → Call Booked → Call Held → Proposal Sent → Won/Lost) | Standard retainer funnel; clear conversion measurement |
| Retainer term | 6 months minimum, then month-to-month, 30-day notice | Industry-standard for retainer agencies; protects against early churn |
| Stripe billing model | `send_invoice` for first month, auto-charge after | Friendlier to contractors who like seeing paperwork before card |
| Cancellation handling | Email Adam to cancel (not self-serve in portal) | Forces a save-the-relationship conversation; revisit at month 12 |
| Norse imagery frequency | Used 2-3 times total across files | "Sparingly" per the brief |

---

## Items that still need YOU (not me)

The brief said "do not attempt to create accounts, enter payment info, or modify DNS." So these are blocking items the kit can't complete:

- [ ] File FL LLC (SunBiz.org, ~$125)
- [ ] Get EIN (IRS.gov, free, 10 min)
- [ ] Open business banking (Mercury/Relay)
- [ ] Book and complete FL startup attorney consult (30 min, ~$300-500)
- [ ] Register 3 domains in Cloudflare
- [ ] Set up 2 Google Workspace tenants
- [ ] Configure DNS for all 3 domains
- [ ] Decide on `{{PHYSICAL_ADDRESS}}` solution (PO Box recommended)
- [ ] Provision Hetzner VPS + deploy stack
- [ ] Sign up for all Tier 2/3 tools per `tools-and-accounts.md`
- [ ] Replace all `PLACEHOLDER_*` values in the n8n workflow JSONs with real IDs
- [ ] Paste full system prompts into the relevant n8n HTTP node bodies
- [ ] Get attorney sign-off on the retainer agreement before first client send
- [ ] Set up monitoring (UptimeRobot or Uptime Kuma)
- [ ] Set up business credit card and run all agency expenses through it from day 1
- [ ] Open Wave Accounting and start logging transactions weekly

---

## Files that are explicitly DRAFTS

- `/04-Client-Templates/retainer-agreement-v1.md` — clearly labeled. **Do not send to any client without attorney review.**

Everything else is intended to be immediately usable, though all docs improve with iteration as you learn what's actually true vs. what was a hypothesis.

---

## How to evolve this system

1. **Weekly metrics + monthly retros are the feedback loop.** Don't skip them.
2. **When you change the playbook, change the doc.** If you stop using a tool, delete or archive its setup file. If a sequence variant becomes the default, update `polk-contractor-sequence-v1.md` and create v2.
3. **Agent rule files are living documents.** After each monthly retro, propose changes to the 5 rule files based on what you learned. The agents only stay useful if you keep them current.
4. **Version everything important.** When you make a meaningful change to a prompt, sequence, or workflow, copy the old version to a `/archive/YYYY-MM/` folder before editing.

---

## One final note

This system is overbuilt for month 1 and probably under-built for month 12. That's by design. The brief asked for a complete starting kit, not the steady-state. You'll add and subtract as the business teaches you what it actually wants to be.

The thing that won't change: the daily review, the weekly metrics, the monthly retro. Those are the muscle. Tools come and go; the discipline of looking honestly at the numbers is what compounds.

Now go build it.

— end of summary —
